children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to recognize, encourage, and promote the use of comprehensive work-learning-service programs as a valuable educational approach when it is an integral part of the institution’s educational program and a part of a financial plan which decreases reliance on grants and loans.

children.heading: Source and use of funds
children.label: b Source and use of funds

children.heading: Source of funds
children.label: 1 Source of funds
children.text: In addition to the sums appropriated under subsection (f) of this section, funds allocated to the institution under this part and part E of this subchapter may be transferred for use under this section to provide flexibility in strengthening the self-help-through-work element in financial aid packaging.

children.label: A
children.text: support the educational costs of qualified students through self-help payments or credits provided under the work-learning-service program of the institution within the limits of part F of this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: promote the work-learning-service experience as a tool of postsecondary education, financial self-help and community service-learning opportunities;

children.label: C
children.text: carry out activities described in section 1087–53 or 1087–56 of this title;

children.label: i
children.text: community-based work-learning-service alternatives that expand opportunities for community service and career-related work; and

children.label: ii
children.text: alternatives that develop sound citizenship, encourage student persistence, and make optimum use of assistance under this part in education and student development;

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate and carry out joint projects and activities to promote work service learning; and

children.label: F
children.text: carry out a comprehensive, longitudinal study of student academic progress and academic and career outcomes, relative to student self-sufficiency in financing their higher education, repayment of student loans, continued community service, kind and quality of service performed, and career choice and community service selected after graduation.

children.heading: Application
children.label: c Application
children.text: Each eligible institution may submit an application for funds authorized by subsection (f) of this section to use funds under subsection (b)(1) of this section at such time and in such manner as the Secretary, by regulation, may reasonably require.

children.heading: Match required
children.label: d Match required
children.text: Funds made available to work-colleges pursuant to this section shall be matched on a dollar-for-dollar basis from non-Federal sources.

children.label: A
children.text: has been a public or private nonprofit, four-year, degree-granting institution with a commitment to community service;

children.label: B
children.text: has operated a comprehensive work-learning-service program for at least two years;

children.label: C
children.text: requires students, including at least one-half of all students who are enrolled on a full-time basis, to participate in a comprehensive work-learning-service program for at least five hours each week, or at least 80 hours during each period of enrollment, except summer school, unless the student is engaged in an institutionally organized or approved study abroad or externship program; and

children.label: D
children.text: provides students participating in the comprehensive work-learning-service program with the opportunity to contribute to their education and to the welfare of the community as a whole; and

children.label: A
children.text: is an integral and stated part of the institution’s educational philosophy and program;

children.label: B
children.text: requires participation of all resident students for enrollment and graduation;

children.label: C
children.text: includes learning objectives, evaluation, and a record of work performance as part of the student’s college record;

children.label: D
children.text: provides programmatic leadership by college personnel at levels comparable to traditional academic programs;

children.label: E
children.text: recognizes the educational role of work-learning-service supervisors; and

children.label: F
children.text: includes consequences for nonperformance or failure in the work-learning-service program similar to the consequences for failure in the regular academic program.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
